chapter: Chapter 1: The Story of Village Palampur

questions.question: What is the main production activity in village Palampur?
questions.answer: Farming is the main production activity in Palampur.

questions.question: Name the four factors of production.
questions.answer: The four factors of production are land, labour, physical capital, and human capital.

questions.question: What are the two types of physical capital?
questions.answer: The two types of physical capital are fixed capital and working capital.

questions.question: Give an example of fixed capital.
questions.answer: Tools, machines, and buildings are examples of fixed capital.

questions.question: Give an example of working capital.
questions.answer: Raw materials and money in hand are examples of working capital.

questions.question: What is meant by multiple cropping?
questions.answer: Multiple cropping is growing more than one crop on a piece of land during the year.

questions.question: During which season is Kharif crops grown?
questions.answer: Kharif crops are grown during the rainy season.

questions.question: Name the crops grown in the Kharif season in Palampur.
questions.answer: Jowar and bajra are the main Kharif crops grown in Palampur.

questions.question: During which season is Rabi crops grown?
questions.answer: Rabi crops are grown during the winter season.

questions.question: Which crop is primarily grown in Palampur during the Rabi season?
questions.answer: Wheat is the main crop grown during the Rabi season.

questions.question: What was the main purpose of the Green Revolution?
questions.answer: The Green Revolution aimed to increase agricultural production using HYV seeds and modern methods.

questions.question: What is the full form of HYV seeds?
questions.answer: The full form of HYV is High Yielding Varieties.

questions.question: What is the standard unit of measuring land in villages?
questions.answer: Bigha or Guintha are local units, but Hectare is the standard unit.

questions.question: Who provides labour for small farmers?
questions.answer: Small farmers and their families provide their own labour.

questions.question: Who provides labour for medium and large farmers?
questions.answer: Medium and large farmers hire farm labourers to work on their fields.

questions.question: How do small farmers arrange for capital?
questions.answer: Small farmers borrow money from large farmers or moneylenders at high interest rates.

questions.question: How do large farmers arrange for capital?
questions.answer: Large farmers use their own savings from the sale of surplus farm produce.

questions.question: Define surplus in farming.
questions.answer: Surplus is the difference between total production and the amount kept for family consumption.

questions.question: Name one non-farm activity in Palampur.
questions.answer: Dairy is a common non-farm activity in Palampur.

questions.question: What is the nearest large village to Palampur?
questions.answer: Raiganj is the nearest large village to Palampur.

questions.question: Name the small town near Palampur.
questions.answer: Shahpur is the small town near Palampur.

questions.question: What is the main drawback of modern farming methods?
questions.answer: Modern farming methods often lead to the loss of soil fertility and depletion of groundwater.

questions.question: Why do wages of farm labourers in Palampur vary?
questions.answer: Wages vary due to the type of work, duration, and high competition for work among labourers.

questions.question: Mention one transport service available in Palampur.
questions.answer: Rickshaws, tongas, jeeps, and tractors are used for transport services.

questions.question: What is the role of human capital in production?
questions.answer: Human capital is the knowledge and enterprise needed to put together land, labour, and physical capital.
